Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Infinitive verb phrase with object; metaphorical motion clause
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To rush into speaking or acting before thinking
Usage Contexts: daily speech, family advice, workplace discussions
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Pastoral imagery of goats running ahead of a herd causing disorder
First Example Sentence: Tawe, walikusenda mbuli kumbele lyabbala, toyeyi cintu coonse.
Second Example Sentence: Bamwi balikusenda mbuli kumbele lyabbala mu nkani zya muunzi.
Third Example Sentence: Mukwesu wangu uyo ulakusenda mbuli kumbele lyabbala lyonse.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To pretend to be wise in front of others
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Village assemblies where speakers sometimes exaggerate wisdom
First Example Sentence: Wakomba maano kumbele aa bantu pele tabaambi kuti waziba.
Second Example Sentence: Abana bamwe balakomba maano kumbele aa balo.
Third Example Sentence: Utakombi maano kumbele aa bantu, baakuziba.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To feel deep worry or distress
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Heart as metaphorical seat of worry in Tonga thought
First Example Sentence: Ndilakulya mwoyo nkaambo mwana wangu taloolekwi.
Second Example Sentence: Baama balakulya mwoyo nkaambo aa mapenzi aa muunzi.

Second Example Sentence: Uyo wafumbwa munwe mu mulilo mu milandu ya bantu.
Third Example Sentence: Bamwi balafumbwa munwe mu mulilo pele tabeezi.

First Example Sentence: Wakaka bbala lyangu kumbele aa bantu.
